content:
oof, this has a couple more problems than at first glance. It seems the text lines from the WoD descriptions (State\Desc) got copy-pasted onto the lines for the in-battle tooltip descriptions (State\WeaponDesc) which will completely break the tooltips because they're using the wrong Rich Text Tags...
Taking "Drifting in Desire" for example:
> EN State\Desc
> Team Unique: The wielder's counter is increased by `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg3]%>`. For each attack received, gain Temporary `<PowerIconKeywords:STR>` equal to `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg1]%>` of the wielder's ATK. After the wielder uses Exalt, gain counter equal to `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg2]%>` of the wielder's ATK.
> EN State\WeaponDesc
> The wielder's Counter DMG is increased by `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg3]%>`. For each attack received, gain `<WeaponEffect_Num:[Power:DescArg1]>` Temporary `<PowerIconKeywords:STR>`. After the wielder uses Exalt, gain `<WeaponEffect_Num:[Counterattack:DescArg2]>` Counter.
I can see where the problem probably started, because the first wheel listed in the sheet (Memory Spiral) had the same RTTs in both Descriptions, but not all of them are like that. In this case,
- `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg3]%>` stays as `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg3]%>`,
- `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg1]%>` becomes `<WeaponEffect_Num:[Power:DescArg1]>`, and
- `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg2]%>` becomes `<WeaponEffect_Num:[Counterattack:DescArg2]>`

> ID State\Desc
> *Counter* pengguna bertambah sebanyak `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg3]%>`. Setiap kali menerima serangan, dapatkan `<PowerIconKeywords:STR>` sebanyak `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg1]%>` dari ATK pengguna. Setelah pengguna melepaskan *Exalt*, dapatkan *Counter* sebanyak `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg2]%>` dari ATK pengguna.
So the WeaponDesc for this needs to be:
> ID State\WeaponDesc
> ```Counter pengguna bertambah sebanyak <WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg3]%>. Setiap kali menerima serangan, dapatkan <PowerIconKeywords:STR> sebanyak <WeaponEffect_Num:[Power:DescArg1]> dari ATK pengguna. Setelah pengguna melepaskan Exalt, dapatkan Counter sebanyak <WeaponEffect_Num:[Counterattack:DescArg2]> dari ATK pengguna.```
I would close the pull request until this is fixed

another thing that needs to be noted is when the two are fundamentally structured differently than each other.
I just spent about an hour trying to help by just copy-pasting the correct Args into place, but some don't correspond correctly, and sadly I don't speak Indonesian so I can't help structure the wording.

Taking "Heart of a Knight" for example:
> EN State\Desc
> Team Unique: The shield generated by the wielder's "Exalt" is increased by `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg2]%>`. After the wielder releases "Exalt", they obtain Temporary `<PowerIconKeywords:STR>` equal to `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg1]%>` of the wielder's DEF. If your team is in a `<FragileIconKeywords:Fragile>` state, the effect is doubled.
> EN State\WeaponDesc
> The wielder's "Exalt" increases Shield Generation by `<WeaponEffect_Num:[StateArg2]%>`. After the wielder releases "Exalt", they obtain `[Power:DescArg1]` Temporary `<PowerIconKeywords:STR>`. If team is in a `<FragileIconKeywords:Fragile>` state, the effect is doubled.

If I had just copy-pasted the Args from EN State\WeaponDesc into the erroneous ID State\"Weapon"Desc, assuming (bc I don't have real stats on hand) 120 DEF, 15% DEF-to-STR ratio, and 10% Exalt Shield buff, in battle it would end up reading like this:
> "Shield yang dihasilkan dari “Exalt” pengguna bertambah 10%. Setelah menggunakan “Exalt”, berikan tambahan STR sementara sebanyak 18 dari DEF pengguna. Jika tim dalam kondisi Fragile, efek dilipat-gandakan."

Which, I don't know if it sounds as bad in Indonesian as it does in English, but...
> Team Unique: The shield generated by the wielder's "Exalt" is increased by 10%. After the wielder releases "Exalt", they obtain Temporary STR equal to 18 of the wielder's DEF. If your team is in a Fragile state, the effect is doubled.
Is REALLY bad...

I'm realizing the "Translators first please" shit-uation we have going on (see <#1419179851272814602>) is really relevant to any team that's translating *from english* to their language... Especially in the case of Gameplay stuff

<@183782216353054720> I just regain my consciousness..
and, thanks for the feedback update...

one thing that I fear is changing words like those that I found from the sheet, therefore I didn't change them. xD

but.. again, thanks for pointing it out.. we ( I ), will fix those one at a time..

and while we're at it, does the lore translation have any issue?

If no, I will erase all the kits and only upload the lore first, so at least ID have something changes.

The lore should be fine (as fine as the rest of the story in the game, which is... MTL quality), the problem with translating Gameplay text is you have to be extra careful to make sure that `<code:sections>` end up displaying properly in context.

I'll make a guide (maybe tonight, probably tomorrow) on what I understand about (a) the rich text tags and how they work, and (b) the processes that these files go through to make it into the game, and what could be lost in conversion if not careful.

one thing I noticed was that in your pull request message you uploaded the .csv version of the .xlsx you uploaded, and it kinda... borke. 

Make sure you delete the column (yes, the entire column, not just its contents) that has all of the merged cells that note the name of each WoD, as that isn't supposed to be in the file when they go upstream, and make sure the columns in the file are these, in this order:

Key, ShortKey, Source, Row, Module, Field, Character, CN, ID

And I'm not sure what program you're using, or why it saved this way, but that .csv file was delimited by semicolons `;` instead of commas `,` so make sure it's saved as UTF-8 .csv (comma separated values) <:BennettThumbsUp:773345984474120202> <:celeste_pray:1408324281624170566>

Gotcha..
imma come for a consult for every now and then... xD
Please do! I never mind answering questions, especially if it means making sure things go smoothly!!
